plugin_desc: Alcatel-Lucent Enterprise OmniSwitch (AOS) switch-specific SNMP collection (ALCATEL-IND1-HEALTH-MIB, IANA PEN 6486). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects OmniSwitch private OIDs for device health: 1-minute average CPU utilization (healthDeviceCpu1MinAvg) and memory utilization (healthDeviceMemoryLatest), both reported directly as percentages. Temperature lives in the chassis hardware table and fan/power-supply state in the chasEntPhysical entity table with per-entity-type row filtering that telegraf inputs.snmp cannot do, so those are N/A. Supported brands/models — Alcatel-Lucent Enterprise OmniSwitch (OS6xxx/OS9xxx, AOS6/AOS8; enterprise 6486); distinct from Switch Alcatel-Lucent SNMP which covers Nokia/ALU 7750 SR service routers.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the ZTE ZXR10 device control plane (ZXR10-MIB zxr10SystemCpuUtility5m, five-minute average), averaged across units and normalized to 0-100. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the ZTE ZXR10 device, read directly as a percent-used value (ZXR10-MIB zxr10SystemMemUsed). High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
